parsed_review.summary: CP00-466 is a descriptor-only analytics-core pack (RP15.P06.M03.S15-RP15.P06.M04.S02, risk A, 10 units) and its descriptor-verified claim is fully supported. Pack binding, requirements (deliverable {ui:1,security_audit:4,test:4,implementation:1}; micro-title {Primary:8,Secondary:2}; section rows), and range are identical across manifest, review-baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json, the contract current_pack/p06_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor projection, and the registry binding. The service builders (createAnalyticsCoreCp466P06ImplementationWorkflowSliceCaseSet/Descriptor) and validators (Coverage, Descriptor, HermesEvidencePacket, ClaudeReviewPacket, CloseoutHandoff) construct only Object.freeze static descriptors with no I/O, DB, network, or runtime dispatch; the freeze wrapper pins every no-write/no-leak flag and each row asserts descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included=false. All 7 required_public_exports are defined (service.js + validators.js) and imported from index.js in model.test.js (3 CP466 tests covering coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority/evidence boundaries). The read-only validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs deep-equals contract sections to the registry descriptors, enforces the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, no enterprise trust from local validation), pins historical_pack_bindings tail to CP00-466, and preserves CP453-465 coverage (no regression, no premature CP467 implementation). production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false correctly reflect the descriptor layer; the production_ready gate is not weakened. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer and Hermes stays descriptor-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded in product runtime. I could not execute the validation commands (read-only, no Bash); their pass status is taken from command-evidence.json, but the underlying source those commands check is structurally consistent. Two non-blocking P3 notes only: the three post-review artifacts are not yet present (expected sequencing) and a harmless categorization overlap in the harness changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack may proceed to the post-review hardening steps.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts not yet present (expected pre-review sequencing)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts and every unit's evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-466/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json exist at review time (confirmed via Glob). These three are produced downstream of this read-only review in the hardening sequence (read_only_claude_run -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection). Pack status is correctly 'implemented_pending_review' and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with valid_review_receipts empty, so this is the expected pre-review state, not a defect in the pack.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are generated from this accepted review and pass npm run closeout-pack:validate before commit; closeout must remain gated until all five required artifacts exist.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists two paths in both untracked-implementation and unrelated-preserved arrays
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-466/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. active_pack_required_untracked_files is [] and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is [], so neither path is treated as active pack scope and the descriptor-only claim is unaffected; the dual categorization is only mildly ambiguous and does not match the manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (which omits these two).
findings.recommendation: Normalize the harness scope output so a path is categorized as either pack-adjacent untracked or unrelated-preserved, not both. No product code or descriptor impact.
